examples: He was brought up in a strict Calvinist household.
This is particularly true of the Calvinist tradition.
His father was a professor of medieval studies and his mother a strict Calvinist.
Woodrow Wilson, a Calvinist, believed he was one of the elect, chosen to lead, teach and inspire.
For a city with such a strong Calvinist streak, Edinburgh's boozers are astonishingly sybaritic.
